text.title: GitHub Projects API and Agent Rules
text.summary: GraphQL API rules, gh CLI patterns, and agent-specific gotchas for GitHub Projects v2.

rule.statement: Store project_id (PVT_kwDO...), field_ids for Status/Priority/Sprint, and option_ids for field values in .aidocs/memory/ or constitution.md after the first fetch. GraphQL mutations require these IDs on every call — re-fetching them each session wastes API quota and rate limits.
rule.rationale: GraphQL mutations need node_ids (not human names). A project with 50 items consumes 50-100 GraphQL points per query; at 5000 points/hour limit, repeated ID lookups exhaust the quota.

antipattern.description: Agent fetches project_id and field_ids at the start of every operation without caching.
antipattern.reason: Each GraphQL query consumes 50-100 of the 5000 points/hour rate limit. Caching IDs reduces overhead to zero for subsequent sessions.

rule.statement: Use `gh project` subcommands as the primary agent interface for standard operations: item-list, item-add, item-edit. Fall back to raw GraphQL only for operations not covered by the CLI (e.g., custom field creation).
rule.rationale: gh CLI abstracts GraphQL complexity and handles authentication transparently. Direct GraphQL mutations require explicit token management.

example.code:
# List current sprint items gh project item-list 1 --owner myorg --format json \ | jq '.items[] | select(.fieldValues[] | .name == "In Progress")'
# Move item to In Review gh project item-edit --id PVTI_xxx --project-id PVT_xxx \ --field-id PVTF_xxx --single-select-option-id PVTO_xxx

rule.statement: Use PROJECT_TOKEN (classic token with project scope) for GitHub Actions workflows that write to Projects. The default GITHUB_TOKEN does not have project scope — Actions using it will fail silently on project field updates.
rule.rationale: Missing project scope on GITHUB_TOKEN is the most common cause of silent project automation failures in CI.

rule.statement: Rate limit: 5000 GraphQL points per hour per token. Complex project queries consume 50-100 points each. Monitor usage for boards with more than 200 items; consider caching project state locally between queries.
rule.rationale: Large open-source projects with many items can exhaust the hourly GraphQL quota in a single automated triage pass.

rule.statement: Use milestones for release targets in addition to project iteration fields. Milestones are fully accessible via `gh milestone` CLI and REST API, and provide a release-scoped view that project boards cannot replicate.
rule.rationale: Project iteration fields show sprint scope; milestones show release scope. Both are needed: sprint for team execution, milestone for stakeholder communication.

text.reference: Feature issue template (YAML form with project auto-assign)
GitHub Actions workflow for project field automation
